topics.title: Dental Procedures and Medical Fears
topics.summary: The studio explores the complexities of dental work, personal anxieties regarding medical professionals, and the influence of parental fears on individual health choices.

topics.title: Morning Hygiene and Upcoming Vegas Events
topics.summary: The studio discusses a guest's lack of morning dental hygiene, the logistics of upcoming promotional events in Las Vegas, and the specific production requirements for filming at club openings.

topics.title: Football Prediction Contest and Guest Interactions
topics.summary: The studio hosts a football prediction contest with various contestants, including Alice, Bigfoot, and Elliot Offen, while engaging in heated exchanges and promotional segments.

topics.title: New York Post Reporting Controversy
topics.summary: The studio discusses a New York Post article by John Manelli that falsely claimed Howard Stern was considering returning to terrestrial radio, leading to a debate over journalistic integrity and the reporter's consulting ties.

topics.title: Vehicle Damage and Outsourced Customer Service
topics.summary: The studio discusses the destruction of vehicles in floodwaters, the difficulties of navigating outsourced customer service lines in India, and a former colleague's conflict with a radio station.

topics.title: Radio Career Conflicts and Interview Anecdotes
topics.summary: The show features a discussion regarding past radio partnerships, the challenges of securing interviews with high-profile guests like Larry Flynt, and humorous accounts of Gary Garver's controversial interview tactics.

topics.title: Comedy Standards and Industry Anecdotes
topics.summary: The studio explores the evolution of comedy, the aging of classic comedians like Dick Van Dyke, and the distinction between clean and blue humor in television and stand-up.

topics.title: Film Reviews and Celebrity Commentary
topics.summary: The studio features a series of film reviews by Steve Grillo, including critiques of Artie Lange's Beer League and Lucky Number 11, alongside discussions regarding celebrity behavior and show business.

topics.title: Homeless Lap Dance Competition
topics.summary: The studio hosts a segment featuring models from a website that offers lap dances to men who are down on their luck, followed by an upcoming competition where three homeless men will share their stories to win a dance.

topics.title: Interviews with Homeless Men
topics.summary: The studio hosts interview three homeless men to determine who has the saddest story in order to win a lap dance from two women.

topics.title: Homeless Men and Lap Dances
topics.summary: The show features a segment where three homeless men are selected by the studio staff to receive lap dances from women while being offered drinks and cigarettes.

topics.title: Artie's Struggle with Heroin Addiction
topics.summary: The show features Artie discussing his history with heroin addiction, the role of a specific medication in his recovery, and the impact of substance abuse on his professional life.

topics.title: Addiction Recovery and Celebrity News
topics.summary: The studio features a candid conversation regarding one guest's journey through heroin addiction, his use of Subutex for recovery, and the personal toll on his family before transitioning into a segment on celebrity news stories.

topics.title: Heroin Addiction and Celebrity Philanthropy
topics.summary: The studio explores the personal experiences of addiction to heroin, its physical effects, and various slang terms while transitioning into news regarding celebrity donations to charity.

topics.title: Radio Show Segment
topics.summary: The studio features a series of segments including listener feedback on personal conduct, discussions regarding vehicle purchases and satellite radio, reports on international relations and Iraq's security situation, and commentary on celebrity news.

topics.title: Celebrity News and Show Updates
topics.summary: The show covers various celebrity news items, including a divorce, Tiger Woods' response to media reports regarding his wife, Clay Aiken's refusal to address rumors about his sexuality, and updates on upcoming performances and television appearances.
